title: Southeast Asia Semiconductor — Consultant Reading Frame
lens: Built outside-in, from public data only, from the viewpoint of an external industry-research consultant advising a specialty (mature-node) foundry with an existing Singapore anchor. No UMC-internal customer, capacity, or financial data is used.
structural_finding: None of the five countries (Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, Philippines) has a confirmed commercial 300mm front-end wafer fab. The region's only front-end anchor is Singapore. For a specialty foundry, the 'Southeast Asia ex-Singapore' question is therefore not a front-end siting question — it is a back-end adjacency, demand-sensing, and China+1 optionality question.
how_to_read: Modules are ordered by decision driver, not by data category. Demand and competition (Modules 2-3) carry the most decision weight and are the least documented; infrastructure facts (Module 5) are the best documented but move the decision least, so they are deliberately demoted and every figure is quality-tagged.

quality_legend.label: Comparable / directly citable
quality_legend.definition: Source, year and definition are clear; safe for management-brief figures.

quality_legend.label: Verified but not cross-comparable
quality_legend.definition: Real data, but definitions differ across countries/years; use for background and trend reading.

quality_legend.label: Estimate / Proxy
quality_legend.definition: Range, non-semiconductor role, or secondary source; screening only, not precise financial modelling.

quality_legend.label: Author judgment / data gap
quality_legend.definition: No unified public quantitative data, or analytical narrative; a discussion prompt requiring later verification.

knowledge_note: Competitive and demand items (Modules 2-3) were verified against primary company/government sources in July 2026 — GF and VSMC (Singapore), Infineon (Kulim/Thailand), Amkor (Vietnam), Malaysia NSS, and ARM x Danantara are now A/B. Remaining C/D items are genuine estimates or public-data gaps, not unverified claims.
